name: View Transitions — Suspense, nesting, coexistence
description: Suspense reveals: string `enter`/`exit` classes (not type maps) on fallback vs content VTs; content VT uses `default="none"`. Directional page VT (type-keyed) + Suspense reveal can coexist — different moments; both should default-none. Multiple VTs in one transition fire together. Parent VT exiting suppresses nested child VT enter/exit (outermost wins) — no per-item stagger during page nav today. Use `default="none"` liberally to prevent unrelated cross-fades (revalidations, deferred search).

metadata.intent: Layer Suspense and page transitions without cross-interference

must_use: Simple string classes for suspense-driven reveals
must_not: Put directional type-keyed VT in a persistent layout wrapping `{children}` when pages also use VT (nested limitation / silent no-op)
conventions: Directional slides only for hierarchical or ordered sequences; lateral tabs → fade or none.

steps.description: Wrap skeleton in `<ViewTransition exit="slide-down">` (or chosen recipe) and content in `<ViewTransition enter="slide-up" default="none">`.
Place directional transitions in page components, not shared layouts that never unmount.
If you need per-item enter/exit during a route transition, account for current nested VT limitation (only outermost animates) or restructure.
